title: Hawking Condensation Receipt
description: Schema v0.2 (extends docs/plans/studio_maximization_2026_06_27.md §19.3 v0.1). The smallest publishable unit of a Hawking claim: one condensation_receipt.json + one quality card. Every Studio run emits one. See §20.3 for the eight invalidation rules enforced by receipt_verify.py.

receipt_version.description: Schema version. This file encodes v0.2.

repro_level.description: §20.6 reproducibility gradient. REQUIRED — no claim without it. R0 private; R1 author-rerunnable; R2 artifact identified+measured; R3 one-command same-machine-class repro (minimum bar for a public WIN); R4 third-party Mac (trust moat); R5 the format itself is cited externally.

claim_type.description: What this receipt asserts. density = smaller at near-parent quality (both fit). cliff = loads+serves where Q4 cannot. scale-point = a point on the bit-floor curve. negative = a published failure. baseline = a sanity/baseline measurement that never sets a verdict (e.g. 0.5B).

machine.description: Human-readable machine string, e.g. 'Mac Studio M2 Max, 96GB unified, 2TB'.

machine_class.description: Canonical class token for the §20.6 R3 same-class repro, e.g. 'M2Max-96', 'M3Pro-18'.

source_model.description: Exact HF id or local path of the bf16/f16 parent.

source_sha256.description: sha256 of the source weights — identity, not trust-me. REQUIRED (invalidation rule 4).

condensed_artifact.description: Path or HF id of the condensed artifact. For claim_type=baseline this may name the measured metrics file rather than a separate weights blob.

artifact_sha256.description: sha256 of the condensed weights / measured artifact — output hash (§19.8 #5). REQUIRED (invalidation rule 4).

effective_bpw.description: Baker AGGREGATE bpw (RHT + outlier + residual + side-info), never nominal. REQUIRED and must be > 0 (invalidation rule 1).

nominal_bpw.description: Reported too, but never the headline.

peak_rss_gb.description: Peak resident memory of serving, on the named machine.

condense_peak_rss_gb.description: Peak RSS during condensation — the out-of-core proof (§13d).

wall_clock_s.description: Wall-clock seconds for the run (§20.11 cost line).

energy_kwh.description: Estimated energy (label it as estimated). §20.11 'no rented GPU' line.

tokens_per_second.description: Decode tok/s of the condensed artifact.

baseline_q4_load_result.description: Whether the Q4 baseline loaded at all on the named machine.

baseline_q4_tps.description: If Q4 loads, time it — the cliff number.

baseline_best_effort.description: §20-BASELINES honesty flag. A best-effort baseline can support a contingent/negative claim but NEVER a public win (invalidation rule 8).

ppl_parent.description: Perplexity of the f16/bf16 parent on the frozen suite.

ppl_condensed.description: Perplexity of the condensed artifact on the frozen suite.

kl_parent_condensed.description: KL(parent || condensed) on the frozen suite.

multiwindow_n.description: Number of held-out eval windows (§6). For a quality claim this must be >= 4 (invalidation rule 2).

multiwindow_worst_pct.description: The WORST window's ppl-delta-pct, not the mean (§6).

prompt_suite_hash.description: sha256 of the frozen prompt cassette (§20.11).

quality_gate.description: §20-GATES four levels. pass = clears §6 bar (<=+2% ppl, KL in band, tripwire held, R3+). warn = +2-4% band, contingent/portfolio only. fail = misses gate, ships as FAILURES.md. invalid = violates a §20.3 rule; the run does not count and the reason is published in invalidation_reasons.

invalidation_reasons.description: Populated when quality_gate == 'invalid'. Each string names a violated §20.3 rule.

hawking_commit.description: Git sha of the Hawking repo at run time. REQUIRED (invalidation rule 5).

commands.description: Exact reproduce commands. REQUIRED, non-empty (invalidation rule 5).

raw_logs.description: Paths or artifact urls to the raw logs.

mps_headline.description: True if the HEADLINE number was produced on the MPS backend. If true, cpu_bf16_confirmed must also be true (§3, invalidation rule 7).

cpu_bf16_confirmed.description: True if a CPU-bf16 confirmation backs the headline number (§3).
